title: 向英國舉報手持BNO藍絲，大家有責

replies.msg:
我地要建立一個database, 監察大家身邊藍絲嘅行為，要佢地有BNO 都走得無咁易.

投訴template (抄番 https://lihkg.com/thread/2089119/page/39 呢個post)
-----------------------------


Dear Sirs/Madames,

I refer to the decision of the UK Prime Minister on 1 July 2020 to extend the leave to remain in the UK from 6 months to 5 years and thereafter the right to apply for settled status for an extra period of 12 months upon which holders of BN(O) passport will be eligible to apply for British citizenship.

I write to urge your esteemed Office to reconsider the decision to renew the BN(O) passport of 你要舉報果條友個名, (who is either in a relationship with or married to an incumbent staff of the Hong Kong Police Force/ who is supporting police brutality in Hong Kong/ who is supporting Hong Kong/ China's authoritarianism). As you might be aware that such a decision may be seriously contradictory to the underlying rationale behind the bespoke immigration route recently announced. I also attached supporting evidence for your kind consideration.

Thank you for your kind attention.

Best regards,
手足
大家身邊有無撐驚/政苦藍絲

Push

有成5+1年

要所有狗同藍屍賤畜都無得去五眼

推一推先
一齊擁抱大灣區啦

Dear Sirs,

I refer to the decision (or announcement) made by the UK Prime Minister on 1 July 2020 regarding a new immigration approach for BNO passport holders.

你要舉報果條友個名, an applicant of the above mentioned scheme, who is (in a relationship with an incumbent officer of hk police/hk gov dept/supporting police brutality) as the attached evidence shows, should not be granted leave to remain. Hence, I urge the Home Office to reconsider (or pay caution to) his application. Decision to grant him the leave to remain may seriously contradict the underlying rationale behind the bespoke immigration route recently announced.

Thank you for your kind attention.

Best regards,
手足

Dear Sirs,

I refer to the decision (or announcement) made by the UK Prime Minister on 1 July 2020 regarding a new immigration approach for BNO passport holders.

你要舉報果條友個名, an applicant of the above mentioned scheme, who is (in a relationship with an incumbent officer of hk police/hk gov dept/supporting police brutality) as the attached evidence shows, should not be granted leave to remain. Hence, I urge the Home Office to reconsider (or pay caution to) his application. Decision to grant him the leave to remain may seriously contradict the underlying rationale behind the bespoke immigration route recently announced.

Thank you for your kind attention.

Best regards,
手足
